entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Welcome @John23 - I hope you enjoy exploring SAP Community. Be sure to subscribe to SAP Managed Tags for your topic areas of interest!

entry.title: Re: Loops in BTP workflows
entry.content: Thanks @tobias_breyer for the explanation. I am new to BPMN flows. Now I have seen some sample workflows. As far as I understood, there is one flow with 'default' and another flow with a condition going out of the exclusive gateway. If we have to repeat any step or set of steps, we need to simply put the conditioned flow back just before the desired steps.

entry.title: Re: Using Market Segments in Margin Analysis
entry.content:
Hello Catherine,
For Product Group 1, 2,....

You can use a table lookup rule in KEDR with the followings:

Source:
MVKE-MATNR: Material Number, MVKE-VKORG: Sales Organization, MVKE-VTWEG: Distribution Channel, KNVV-SPART: Division
Target:
MVKE-MVGR1: Material group 1, MVKE-MVGR2: Material group 2, ...


For the Customer group1, 2, ...
You can use a a table lookup rule in KEDR with the followings:

Source:
KNVV-KUNNR: Customer Number, KNVV-VKORG: Sales Organization, KNVV-VTWEG: Distribution Channel, KNVV-SPART: Division
Target:
KNVV-KVGR1: Customer group 1, KNVV-KVGR2: Customer group 2, ...

Both of these rules work under Margin-Analysis.
Hope this helps,
Soufiane

entry.title: Call external API from custom action in SAP Fiori Element
entry.content:
Hi,

I've developed a SAP Fiori Elements List report application. I need to call an external API when the user select a row in the list. I've defined a custom action but I don't know how to call external API

The API is the url for trigger a SAP Build Process Automation flow. So the requirement is trigger process automation flow when the user select a row in the list

Anybody can help me?

Thank you.

Piervincenzo.

entry.title: Re: Purchasing group field in quality notifications
entry.content: Thank you, Herman. This I have already checked. Neither the notification type nor any other field has such influence on the Purchasing group field in this IMG option (trs OQM1). It is something with the fact that the notification is created from defect reporting in UD.

entry.title: Re: How to synchronize Set Controlling Hierarchies in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi, 

have you tried the autoreplicate flag in transaction HRY_REPRELEV? 

From the help of the field: 
Changes to group are auto replicated to HRRP

The Auto Replicate flag determines if changes to a set-based group is
automatically synchronized to Hierarchy Runtime Representation (HRRP)
tables.

Use

When a set-based group is set to Report Relevant, an initial version of
runtime hierarchy needs to be generated manually by using the Replicate
Runtime Hierarchy transaction (HRRP_REP). If any further changes are
made to the group later on, you can select one of the following options
to synchronize the changes to the runtime hierarchy:

o In transaction HRY_REPRELEV (Set Report Relevancy), select Auto
Replicate to update the runtime hierarchy each time when the group
is changed and saved.

o In transaction HRRP_REP, re-generate the runtime hierarchy

 

Thanks 

Best 

GIanluca

entry.title: Re: Debug Mode(/H) history
entry.content:
AFAIK, Only for SE16N when using &SAP_EDIT

Read

  • Improvement request Consider SE16N-Changes with &SAP_EDIT in Firefighter Log Synchronization and Review 
  • 3042747 - AC12 : Collect SE16N changes 
  • 3029257 - Enhancement for Firefighter Log Review - Collect SE16N changes

entry.title: How to create AR aging reports using the "G/L Perspective of AR Aging Grid" CDS view
entry.content:
Do you need an overview of overdue receivables sorted by profit center or customer? Do you want to see aggregated overdue amounts by journal entry and journal entry item? Or do you need more aging intervals for your overview than those available in the Overdue Receivables app or the Total Receivables app?
This blog post will show you how to use the G/L Perspective of AR Aging Grid – Query (C_ARAgingGridGenLedgerQry) standard CDS view for this. For more detailed information about the CDS view, see G/L Perspective of AR Aging Grid - Query. 

To use the G/L Perspective of AR Aging Grid – Query standard CDS view for one of the above use cases, you just need to follow this three-part procedure consisting of creating an application, adding this application to a business catalog, and running your custom report:

Part 1: Create Application

  1. Open the View Browser app, search for the C_ARAgingGridGenLedgerQry CDS view, select the CDS view, and choose the Create Application button in the table toolbar.
    The following screenshot shows the search result for C_ARAgingGridGenLedgerQry and highlights the Create Application button.

  2. In the dialog box that appears, enter a report name in the Title field and a description in the Subtitle field.
    The following screenshot shows the dialog box that appears when you choose the Create Application button.

  3.  Choose the OK button.

    Your application is created.
    The following screenshot shows the application ID of your application.

Part 2: Add Application to Business Catalog

  1. To add your application to one or more business catalogs, click the application ID.
  2. In the dialog box that appears, choose the View in Catalog option.
    The Custom Catalog Extensions app opens.
    The following screenshot shows the dialog box.
  3.  In the Custom Catalog Extensions app, choose the Add button in the table toolbar to add your application to one or more business catalogs.
    The following screenshot shows the Used in Business Catalogs results table and highlights the Add button.

  4. In the dialog box that appears, search for the relevant business catalog(s) to add your application to, and choose the OK button.
    The system takes you back to the Used in Business Catalogs results table.
    The following screenshot shows the dialog box that appears when you choose the Add button.

  5. In the Used in Business Catalogs results table, select the business catalog(s) and choose the Publish button in the table toolbar.
    The following screenshot shows the business catalog that you added to your application and highlights the Publish button.
  6. In the confirmation dialog box that appears, choose the OK button.
    Once the status changes from Unpublished to Published as shown in the following screenshot, you are ready to run your custom report.
     

Part 3: Run Custom Report

  1. Log on to the system with a role that includes the business catalog to which you added your application in part two and open the App Finder to search for your custom report.
    The following screenshot shows the App Finder option in the User Actions Menu.
  2. Enter the name of your report in the Search bar. The tile for your application appears.
  3. To add the tile to one or more of your pages, click the + (Plus) sign on the tile as shown in the following screenshot.

  4. In the dialog box that appears, select the pages to which you want to add your application and choose the OK button as shown in the following screenshot.


    You can now find your application directly on the SAP Fiori launchpad as shown in the following screenshot.

     

  5. From here, start the application and run your report.
    The following screenshot shows an example:

Summary
We hope this blog post showed you how easy it is to use the G/L Perspective of AR Aging Grid – Query (C_ARAgingGridGenLedgerQry) standard CDS view to create custom AR aging reports.

If you don’t want to use all the fields defined in the standard CDS view or want to have different fields in the filter bar, we recommend creating a copy of the G/L Perspective of AR Aging Grid – Query (C_ARAgingGridGenLedgerQry) standard CDS view. To do so, you can follow the steps described in the Customer Aging and Supplier Aging Reports at the Line Item Level using Analytical Query in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition blog post.

Feel free to leave a comment on this post below. We’re looking forward to your feedback.

Related Information

G/L Perspective of AR Aging Grid - Query 
View Browser
Custom Catalog Extensions

entry.title: In the "Purge Master Data" job, is there any option to get an attachment file with "Review" option?
entry.content:
In the "Purge Master Data" job, is there any option to get an attachment file with "Review" option?

This way we can check in advance - before running the job without simulation - all affected master data types also during the review execution for "Purge Master Data" job. If we run without "Review" (simulation) option then we can get this detailed log, otherwise not. It will be great having this during the "Review" or "Simulation" execution as well. 

Thanks.

Kind regards,

Sonia

entry.title: SAP CAPM application failed to update data in on-Premise system
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am using SAP CAPM application where frontend is SAP Fiori. I am able to read the HR related data from on-premise system and able to display it in Fiori app. When I tried to modify the data from Fiori and save it back via CAPM application, its failing to update and giving error as below as per logs:

 Error on inserting entry: {"amount":"NaN","startDate":"2024-06-14T08:03:46.330Z","endDate":"2024-06-14T08:03:46.330Z","dateOfOrigin":"2024-06-14T08:03:46.330Z"}

Though, entity type for additional payment is as per below:

entity AdditionalPayment {
    key personnelNumber: String(8) @mandatory not null;
    key payDate: Date;
    amount: Decimal(10,2);
    currency: String;
    wageType: String;
    success: Boolean;
    trancheName: String;
    person: Association to one Person on person.personnelNumber = $self.personnelNumber;
}

If you need anymore details, please let me know and I will be providing it back. 

Please help me on resolving this issue ASAP.

Regards, pankaj

Can I check if the alternate line manager functionality is also planned for 2H 2024? This is something we use heavily so just trying to plan ahead in terms of our own approach, comms, testing etc. 

Thanks

Heidi

entry.title: Re: Is it best practice to assign a Work Order ALWAYS to...
entry.content:
Greetings,

I would say that it depends on in how much detail you want to monitor your processes. The more detail you want the more "deep" in the technical system you need to open your work order. It also depends on the actual process. For example:

- in preventive maintenance you can have regular check on the level of functional location - you would include all equipment that is installed in the functional location. If any breakdown is detected then you can open a breakdown order on the level of specific equipment

- in case of manufacturer prescribed maintenance you would need to plan preventive maintenance in the level of equipment if you do not want to have any warranty issues

- in case of breakdown maintenance it makes sense to open work orders on the level of equipment just so that maintenance personnel know exactly which piece of equipment if faulty

I would say that in general it is best to find balance between administrative overhead (the more work orders you have the more interaction your users will have with the system) and monitoring requirements. 

Bests regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Re: Activation errors while extending Material number len...
entry.content:
Does the dictionary version have CHAR 40 or CHAR 18, before the conversion? What is the delta between active and new version?

Also please check the SAP Note:

2465903 - Error DT064 "Structure change at field level (convert table ...)"

entry.title: Long term absence in org chart
entry.content:
Hello,

we are not using time management in SuccessFactors but we track the employees who are on long term leave in a custom field in job information in EC.

The employees of course stay in the people org chart even they are away for a long time (in some cases even years).

I didn´t find a way so far to not show them without causing other issues (like assigning a dummy manager, etc.)

Do you have any other possible solutions?

Kind regards,

Simon

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration: Understanding “Simple Signer” [3] : verify Signature in Node.js App
entry.content:
In the iFlow javascript file, I would like to use the below two functions to decrypt a private key using forge. How can I use "forge" in the iFlow environment?

 

function loadPrivateKey(privateKeyPem, privateKeyPassphrase) {
    return forge.pki.decryptRsaPrivateKey(privateKeyPem, privateKeyPassphrase);
}
function buildEncodedSignature(privateKey, signingString) {
    let messageDigest = forge.md.sha256.create();
    messageDigest.update(signingString, "utf8");
    let pss = forge.pss.create({
      md: forge.md.sha256.create(),
      mgf: forge.mgf.mgf1.create(forge.md.sha256.create()),
      saltLength: 32
    });
    let signature  = privateKey.sign(messageDigest, pss);
    return base64UrlEncode(signature)
}

entry.title: Re: SAP PM strategy not working as planned
entry.content: what i want is a cycle reset every 6000H. At 8000H should be a 2000H and at 10000H another 4000H ---> I think this will not work since 10000 is not a multiple of 4000. If you want to reset counters you need to do it over measuring documents.

entry.title: Deactivate compound interest for interest calculation on projects with summary settlement
entry.content:
Hi,

we‘re currently working on the concept for the interest calculation for projects with summary settlements.

The challenge right now is to deactivate the compound interest calculation.

We are settling the costs on our projects with two different cost elements. One for the interest, one for all the remaining costs. Now we would like the system to only consider one cost element and not the settled interest on the asset under construction for the interest calculation.

I’d be very thankful for help.

Thank you very much!

#cjz1 #interestcalculation #sapps #projectsystem

Best regards

Have you configured Good Receipt  plugin where you need add Label Document Name and Label Document Version in Print Label Properties.


If yes, then please click on "Generate Preview".

 


Hope this will work for you.

Regards,
Kartik Singhal

entry.title: Re: Handling Unpacked Dangerous Goods in Intra and Interc...
entry.content: Hi Christoph, Thank you for your detailed response. I would like to provide additional context and seek further guidance based on my understanding and the specific scenario I am dealing with. Context: I am dealing with stock transfers within the same company but between plants located in different cities (Intracompany). This requires transportation and thus involves dangerous goods (DG) checks during the process. Specific Scenario: Unpackaged goods, such as a battery (semifinished goods or parts material type), need to be transferred from one plant to another within the same company. These batteries are handled with different material numbers. Packaged goods, such as chainsaws (finished material type), are handled with different material numbers. Challenges: According to the online documents and Learning Hub for S/4 HANA Product complinace, dangerous goods checks can only be successfully processed for products that are packaged. This means that the item on the purchase order must be a packaged product for the DG checks to be performed successfully. Question: Is there a specific configuration or best practice in Product Compliance to handle such scenarios where unpackaged goods like batteries need to be transported safely? Your guidance on these points would be greatly appreciated. Thank you for your assistance and insights. Best regards, Ajit

entry.title: Re: SAP Trail Datasphere Analytical models are not appear...
entry.content:
my tip: Try to isolate the problem.
Is the problem only occuring in that space for that AM or is it ocurring in general, so for all AM of all Spaces ? 
Do you have mulitple DSP connected to the SAC Tenant ? If yes, is the Problem also for other DSP Tenants existing ? 
Did you try to simply copy the AM and save it under a new Name?

entry.title: Re: Inspection plans and Material Masters best practice
entry.content:
Greetings,

I don't think there is any straight answer. If you have materials which are inspected in the same way then you can have 1:N relationship. In this case if you have a change in the inspection plan you only need to change it once.

I would says that you need to answer the following questions:

  • are the materials inspected in the same way 
  • are the materials similar to each other (in terms of material type and procurement type)
  • are the same people/departments responsible for master data maintenance for these materials
  • how often do you update the inspection plan
  • are there any regulatory constraints (change number requirements, GxP, etc.)

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Use the API Anomaly Detection Feature in SAP Integration Suite to Detect Unusual Behavior in APIs
Can I add some sections into my MDK Project
entry.content:
Hi there,

Could you tell me how to create a new Fiori section when I want to classify it into some groups according to the Fiori application pages that I made in the MDK project?

Can any ideas or links help me develop it using sections, like the tiles in the yellow fields?

Thanks for your response.

 

SAP Business Application Studio SAP Fiori SAP Fiori Elements  mobile development kit client

entry.title: Re: FI line item which is linked with Disput case
entry.content:
As the disputed FI Document is attatched to a Case as an Object, you have to: 

Pass the Company Code, FI Document number and Fiscal Year, Line Item in Table IDISPOBJ, in fields COMPANYCODE / ACCOUNTINGDOCUMENT / FISCALYEAR / ACCOUNTINGDOCUMENTITEM.  Then get the unique Case Id. (UUID) from Table field (IDISPOBJ-CASEUNIQUEID).

Then pass the value found in CASEUNIQUEID, in Table SCMG_T_ATTR in field CASE_GUID and get the Dispute Case Id. from Table field (SCMG_T_ATTR-EXT_KEY). 

Kind Regards,

Georgios Sofianos

entry.title: Customer material number to SAP number
entry.content:
Hey guys I'd like to ask someone for help, long story short I have a list of customer material numbers and need a transaction that can search using those numbers to show me what our material numbers are those. Customers have materials under different number as we do. For example ZMATINFO has an option to search using customer material number, but only one at the time, I need to do it for hundreds.

 

Any ideas?

 

Thank you 🙂

 SAP Digital Manufacturing SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights 
@ManoelCosta

Kartik

entry.title: Re: SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more

Thanks for your reply. We have upgraded to S/4HANA 2023 FPS01. SAPUI5 library version is 1.120.15

But still this Copy-Past feature is SAPUI5 tables is not available after upgrade.

Can you please confirm if this feature is indeed available for on-prem S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 customers and if yes under which component I can create a case to SAP to check this issue.

Thanks in advance for your valuable feedback.

Regards

Sundeep

entry.title: Re: T Code to get the data received from external system
entry.content:
Please specify how you receive the data from the external (manufacturing execution system [MES]?!?) systems in the SAP system. Do you use SOA services, BAPIs, IDocs etc.?

Note that there is an RFC-based possibility for the activities you've described (goods issue, goods receipt, batch creation etc.) which is named "process messages". They are part of the so called PI-PCS interface, which is available in SAP ERP and SAP S/4HANA (On Premise/Private Cloud Edition [PCE]). For the (custom) process messages, there is a monitoring transaction (TA CO54 & CO54XT). It allows you to detect issues when receiving the process messages and their processing (that is: failed goods receipt because of foreign key locks etc.). And it allows editing an resending of process messages that could not be properly executed.

entry.title: Re: SAP Trial installation stalling on unbuntu
entry.content: Please check if there is enough space on the file system.

entry.title: Re: Stay Informed About New SAP EarlyWatch Alerts You Are Interested In
entry.content: Hello, thank you for the helpfull information. Is there any possibility to personalize the email notification - e.g. to enrich the emial with the name of the customer account? I have S-Users in several customer accounts and when I get the email notification I only see my name and my S-User and I have to check to which account it belongs ...

entry.title: Re: Differences between SAP ECC and S/4 HANA in HCM
entry.content:
SAP ECC (ERP Central Component) and SAP S/4HANA (Business Suite 4 SAP HANA) are both enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems developed by SAP, but they have significant differences, especially in the Human Capital Management (HCM) module. Here are the key differences:

1. Database and Performance

SAP ECC: Operates on traditional relational databases like Oracle, SQL Server, or IBM DB2. Performance and speed are dependent on the underlying database.
SAP S/4HANA: Runs exclusively on the SAP HANA in-memory database, providing significantly improved performance, real-time data processing, and faster analytics.

2. User Interface

SAP ECC: Uses the traditional SAP GUI (Graphical User Interface), which can be seen as less intuitive and more challenging for new users.
SAP S/4HANA: Utilizes SAP Fiori, a modern, web-based user interface designed for a more intuitive and user-friendly experience, available on various devices.

3. Data Model and Architecture

SAP ECC: Follows a traditional data model with a more complex structure and separate tables for transactions and analytics.
SAP S/4HANA: Simplifies the data model by reducing the number of database tables, using the power of the HANA database to combine transactional and analytical data for real-time insights.

4. Functionality and Features

SAP ECC HCM: Comprehensive but based on older technology, requires additional customization and integration for modern HR processes and capabilities.
SAP S/4HANA HCM (SuccessFactors): Modernized HCM suite known as SAP SuccessFactors. It includes improved functionalities for core HR, talent management, payroll, and analytics. SAP is also focusing on moving HCM capabilities to the cloud with SuccessFactors, providing a more seamless and integrated experience.

5. Integration and Extensibility

SAP ECC: Integration with other systems and modules can be complex and require significant effort.
SAP S/4HANA: Offers better integration capabilities within the SAP ecosystem and with third-party applications, thanks to its advanced APIs and SAP Cloud Platform, making it easier to extend and customize.

6. Deployment and Maintenance

SAP ECC: Typically deployed on-premise, requiring significant hardware, maintenance, and upgrade efforts.
SAP S/4HANA: Can be deployed on-premise, in the cloud, or in a hybrid model. Cloud deployment offers easier maintenance, regular updates, and reduced infrastructure overhead.

7. Innovation and Roadmap

SAP ECC: Limited new feature development as SAP focuses on its next-generation ERP suite.
SAP S/4HANA: Active development with regular updates and new features, ensuring continuous innovation and support from SAP.

8. Migration and Adoption

SAP ECC: Existing customers face significant efforts to upgrade to S/4HANA, involving data migration, process transformation, and user training.
SAP S/4HANA: SAP provides various tools and support for migration, but the process can still be resource-intensive and requires careful planning.

entry.title: Re: Introducing sap.ui.mdc – A New Generation of Smart Controls
entry.content:
Hi @woongkipark !

Unfortunately it is not possible for us to change the visibility for 1.120, as there were some significant changes between 1.124 and 1.120 which we can not port down. Are you bound to 1.120 for any reason?

Best regards

Benedikt

SAPUI5

entry.title: Re: Error en Query - Facturas pagas en plazo (60 días)
entry.content:
Deberías empezar de forma sencilla y luego ir complicando la query visualizando los resultados que ofrece la misma.

Quiero decir, inicias la query con 2 tablas que son las principales y diría casi las únicas que te hacen falta que son VPM2 y OVPM y su relación con las facturas de proveedores y/o abonos OPCH/ORPC

Ahí ya te das cuenta de según sea tu actividad empresarial, la relación va por docentry desde VMP2 a esas otras tablas comentadas, en función del número de líneas que tenga el pago.

Filtras que saque solo pocos registros, bien sea por fecha o proveedor y vas salvando la query a medida que añades otras tablas a la query

Por lo que veo, tienes exceso de tablas en la query para lo que dices que quieres obtener.

entry.title: Re: Objects are imported but not generated when moved to...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can check the transport activation log to see if there are any issues reported while activating the specific objects.

If the logs do not show any issues, you can select each object in the transport (that is not activated) and activate it individually. This is possible for small number of objects. Else try using program RADMASG0 to mass activate objects in one or more specified transports.

Regards,

Kishan

entry.title: Is webdynpro application development outdated?
entry.content: In today's modern era, Do we still need this object in SAP?

entry.title: Re: Outbound Communication to S4 On-Premise(OData) from B...
entry.content:
Hi Vishnu,

as the requests reach your Cloud Connector: Do they also reach your backend system? Any additional logging available there?

The 403 coming from the backend system would usually indicate that the user does not have sufficient authorisations to access the service (authentication worked, otherwise 401 would be returned).

Are you able to directly call the service (e.g. from postman / browser) with the user at the backend system?

Best Regards,

Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP DM for ESRS taxonomies - base taxonomies and temp...
entry.content:
Hello Andrea,

We are not (currently) providing these files yet. as the reporting has been postponed.

You can always create the taxonomy files yourself, based on the published taxonomy and the procedure to get these files into the correct format for DM

Marc

entry.title: Unable to deploy xsuaa service with xs-security.json
entry.content:
Hello! I am following this tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hana-cloud-cap-add-authentication.html 

When running "cf create-service xsuaa application MyHANAApp-auth -c xs-security.json" I get "Incorrect Usage: Invalid configuration provided for -c flag. Please provide a valid JSON object or path to a file containing a valid JSON object. "

The only option that worked was with empty json as inline configuration,

but the xs-security.json file, no matter how I change it crashes with the error above.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration into SAP BW/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Benjamin,
well, a first starting point would be the SAP help for FSM. There is a dedicated area for integration, where you find details for FSM connectors as well.
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_integration/fsm-connector-intro.html

FSM also seems to have a deep integration into S/4HANA. Maybe you can also take the relevant data from there by standard CDS means... worth evaluating.
Regards Frank

entry.content:

I would like to change it with another page, but I'm not sure how. Any suggestions?
I'm sure there's a way. Isn't it ?


Many thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

My name is Steffen and I am from Bavaria, more precisely from the city of Hof in Upper Franconia 🙂

entry.title: Issue with Inter-company purchasing with multiple rates
entry.content:
Many contingent workers have multiple Purchasing Rates (e.g. Specific rate on Internal Projects and higher rate on Customer Project). With that expectation we need more than one PO line for the contingent worker.

We purchase on Cost Center for contingent workers working on an inter-company project. We create the PO on Cost Center, assign a cost rate and an ICO rate to the personnel number in the app “Manage Cost Rates – Services” and the system generates the inter-company invoicing. The contingent worker will then invoice the company where the PO and contract sit.

In that case, only one PO line needs to be valid for the contingent worker, otherwise it will multiply the number of tasks in timesheet app by the number of PO line active. It also multiplies the line if a PO on project also exist, but the system does not prevent time posting. It’s very not convenient for the contingent worker and create a lot of maintenance and support on the back end with creating issues in replication of time coming from the external timesheet system.

Due to the system behavior of duplicating time entries, it creates wrong postings in GRIR accounts, creates reconciliation efforts, creates confusion for the contingent workers that need to send his timesheet, therefore more work on the back end, and we need to prevent this.

The adoption of this influence request would allow us to at least have a workaround for this issue, because Purchasing on Cost Center is also required.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/299853

already referred the below blog

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/external-contingent-workforce-for-customer-projects/ba-p/13565914 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Inter-company purchasing with multiple rates
entry.content: This is new requirement from SAP end, however we request for work arounds if any.

entry.title: Re: Demystifying the Common Super Domain for SAP Mobile Start
entry.content:
Dear @pboulicaut,

Since SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition itself does not require such an activation step, I assume you refer to some other component with your activation / undo question. I would suggest here, to report the issues you are facing directly to the related component with an official support ticket. The responsible colleagues should be able to further assist you on that regards.

Best,
Thomas

Regards,

Andrea

entry.title: Re: Download path of Payment Media in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

The following document should answer your question:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/031c345485b84c8c94265be9ef61d3a8/039683576ea1a96be10000000a4450e5.html?q=payment%20media&locale=en-US:

"With the 'Manage Payment Media" app, you can transfer the data required for electronic payment transactions to banks via a data medium. A payment medium is created with each successful payment run. When you download a payment medium, the app automatically saves it as a .txt or .xml file according to its type."

Please let us know any follow-on questions you may have.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Dates and Roundings in Compensation
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec @pmacgovern and thanks for this extremely informative thread

 

we are facing an issue:

SuccessFactors Employee Central utilizes half-even rounding for the calculation of pay components (as explained in this document). What this means is that it will round "up" or "down" in order to get the closest even number (e.g. 1.036 -> 1.04, 1.029 -> 1.02)

 

But SuccessFactors Compensation cannot utilize half-even rounding, only roundingMode= "up" / "down" / "halfUp"

 

what this means is that when the compensation manager approves the new salary, they will see a value in the Compensation Module that is not the same of the value that will actually be calculated in Employee Central Compensation Information

 

e.g.: an employee has the Yearly Salary = €35.000,00

  • In the Compensation module the Monthly Salary displayed will be €2,916.67 (Yearly Salary / 12)
    • 35.000,00 / 12 = 2,916.666666666667 and it will be rounded up to €2,916.67

  • when the new Yearly Salary is published to EC and the rule is calculating other pay components, Monthly Salary will be calculated to be €2,916.66 
    • 35.000,00 / 12 = 2,916.666666666667 and it will be rounded to the closest even number which is 6

 

my question is: is there a standard procedure to handle this? Or do we have to assume that Monthly Salary calculated in EC will be slightly different than Monthly Salary calculated in the Comp Module?

Is there a best practice to handle this misalignment?

thanks in advance for your time and your help

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

As a data analyst, I have only been involved with SAP as an end user for the last 10 years. I have mostly extracted data from SAP and processed it in Excel.

Now we are starting to build our new BI system based on SAC and I will be responsible for the central creation of the stories.

This is the start of a new chapter for me, where I will continue to expand my knowledge and maybe find some answers to questions that arise here in the community 🙂

entry.title: Production version with validity date in future
entry.content: I have created in ECC a Production Version 2 starting Dec 1st, 2024, I have defined PV 1 to end Nov 30th,2024. In SCM I see planned orders generated only up to nov 30,2024 and it is not recognizing the new PV2. What I did wrong?

entry.title: Re: Is it best practice to assign a Work Order ALWAYS to...
entry.content: Hi Matjaz, thank you for your suggestions, that helps! I am particularly interested in the level where we have (at a specific time) exactly 1 equipment for 1 Functional location. In that case the administrative overhead seems to be the same and it is probably best to relate the order to the EQ. But in case a FL is higher in the hierarchy and contains multiple EQ's, it can make sense to relate the order to the FP, so as to reduce the administrative load.

entry.title: Re: Automatic clearing of exchange rate differences during bank statement upload
entry.content: Hi Eli,is the reported issue by you resolved.Was an enhancement done as suggested by Bohdan.As i am also having same request from Business users to automate the process instead of manual charge off the difference to Forex G/L account.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAPM application failed to update data in on-Premise system
entry.content: I modified the code, now I am getting below error: "cannot insert NULL or update to NULL: PERSONNELNUMBER". I passing all value from CAPM to on-premise system but success as of now. Please find the person entity:- entity Person { key personnelNumber: String(8) @mandatory not null; wageType: String; currency: String; globalId: String; name: String; department: String; participations: Association to many TrancheParticipation on participations.person = $self; recurringPayments: Association to many RecurringPayment on recurringPayments.personnelNumber = $self.personnelNumber }. Your help will be much appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Visualize my code change from ADT local history (Ecli...
entry.content:
Hi Joachim,

thank you for your question! Have you tried selecting "Compare With->Revision History->Class..." from the context menu of a class to compare different revisions in the History view? If you select a revision there, you can compare the code of different revisions.

Kind regards,
Armin

What will be the simplest way to extract those values from payload associated with Message ID?

Many thanks in advance.

P.S. ECC6.

entry.title: Exclusive Temu Coupon Code $100 off {acr897367} or {frg227060}: Get Up To 90% OFF In June 2024
entry.content:
Use this Temu Coupon Code $100 Off {acr897367} or {frg227060} to get exclusive discount of $100 on your first Temu Order + 30% Savings.

The error "HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED" in your GSTR1 RAP application in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud is caused by the maximum number of HTTP client connections being exceeded. This occurs when multiple HTTP requests are generated for an instance and cannot be handled. To resolve this issue, you can try the following:

Reduce the Number of HTTP Requests:
Check if there are any unnecessary HTTP requests being generated in your application. Try to minimize the number of requests by optimizing your code or using more efficient methods.
Increase the Maximum Number of Connections:
You can increase the maximum number of HTTP client connections by configuring the max_connections parameter in your SAP system. However, be cautious when doing so, as it may impact system performance.
Use a More Efficient HTTP Client:
Consider using a more efficient HTTP client library that can handle a larger number of connections without causing the error.
Optimize Your Application:
Review your application's performance and optimize it to reduce the load on the system. This may involve improving database queries, reducing the number of API calls, or using caching mechanisms.
Contact SAP Support:
If none of the above solutions work, you can contact SAP support for further assistance. They may be able to provide more specific guidance or help you troubleshoot the issue.
Remember to carefully evaluate the impact of any changes you make to your system's configuration to ensure that they do not compromise performance or security.

entry.title: SAP BTP Tutorial with HANA DB and FIORI front end with VS code
entry.content:

I am trying to create a CDS project with VS Code that connects to HANA DB, uses XSUAA and exposes data through a FIORI app. Preferably to use multiple FIORI apps that can connect to the same CDS backend service. I tried following a few tutorials from SAP but none of them worked. Is there any clean tutorial / documentation that I can follow to achieve the above scope?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Outbound Communication to S4 On-Premise(OData) from B...
entry.content: Hi Vishnu, do the requests reach the backend? E.g. do you see any entries in SU53 for the user, which would indicate further authorisation issues? I would also suggest to set the principal type to None in your SCC configuration as you are not using principal propagation, but technical authentication. Best Regards, Thomas

Please help!!

Thanks

here is my VM settings
Virtualbox Setup:

entry.title: Re: Unable to open Salesforce Database in Crystal Reports
entry.content:
With Crystal 2020 (64-bit) the report requires a 64-bit ODBC DSN. That DSN is missing on that machine.

Perhaps you have it as a 32-bit DSN but a 64-bit app cannot use a 32-bit DSN.

entry.title: Questions on 'Just Ask' in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

SAP recently launched "Just Ask" for SAC tenants. While exploring its features, I have a few questions that I believe will help me understand its capabilities better.

  1. Contextual Awareness: Does "Just Ask" currently remember context from previous questions and answer new questions based on that context? I suspect this functionality is not yet available. If so, is there a roadmap for its inclusion?
  2. Customizable LLM Model: I understand "Just Ask" utilizes a large language model (LLM) in the background. Is it possible to train this model with my company's specific data to improve its understanding of our unique terminology and data landscape?
  3. "Just Ask" vs. "Joule": I've seen videos showcasing "Joule" and its functionality within the SAC environment. Could you clarify how "Joule" will differ from "Just Ask"? Will "Just Ask" remain available after "Joule" is introduced?
  4. Underlying LLM Model: Which specific LLM model powers "Just Ask"?

Regards,

Animikh
